MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Continued To May-21-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-24-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Corey Bennet of the law offices of Scott Cole & Associates, APC (510-891-9800) appears by telephone as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Jeremy T. Naftel of the law offices of Carothers DiSante & Freudenberger LLP (916-361--0991) appears by telephone as counsel for defendant/opposing party

Both sides orally stipulate to judge pro tem. Clerk signs stipulation with permission. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF SUE TRAYLING'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. First, Defendant is ordered to produce all non-privileged documents that refer to Plaintiff in response to Plaintiff's Request 1. Defendant has not shown a lack of clarity or undue burden in producing all responsive documents referring to Plaintiff, and Calcor Space Facility, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 53 Cal. App. 4th 224-225 does not support Defendant's position here. As to Request No. 4, Defendant is ordered to produce responsive documents subject to the execution of an appropriate form of protective order, as referenced here. As to Request 5, the Court cannot determine whether Plaintiff and Defendant have agreed to the scope of the Request, and if so, whether Defendant has produced all responsive documents, and hence cannot determine whether a specific order is needed with respect to Request 5. Second, based upon Defendant's representations, it appears that Defendant has responded in full to the extent of Plaintiff and Defendant's agreement as to the scope of Form Interrogatory 12.1, and Special Interrogatories 1 and 5; accordingly, there is nothing further to compel as to those requests. Third, it appears that a protective order is appropriate to protect the confidentiality of certain business-related information that is indeed proprietary and confidential. Plaintiff and defense counsel are to meet and confer promptly for the purpose of crafting an appropriate form of confidentiality agreement, and Defendant has the responsibility to affirm and assure that materials designated for confidential treatment are in fact treated and maintained as confidential or proprietary information. For example and without limitation, material otherwise distributed to third parties, the public or otherwise made available without confidentiality agreements in place may well not be entitled to treatment as confidential here, and Plaintiff may seek appropriate relief from this Court for the improper designation of material as confidential or proprietary that do not warrant those designations. Fourth, plaintiff's motion and defendant's motion for monetary sanctions are denied. The moving party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge Pro Tem: Mark LeHocky; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
